commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a8620ace837b1d2ad31be0ca8d8030b42da81777)
commands.review_summary:
The PR widens `isExecutableFile` to accept an optional env and threads that env through executable resolution in `src/infra/executable-path.ts`.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main can generate candidates from caller PATHEXT and then reject the selected file extension against process.env PATHEXT. I did not run a Windows runtime repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action needs contributor Windows proof and maintainer judgment on sibling resolver scope, so this is not queued for automated repair.

target.title: fix(core): propagate caller env PATHEXT through isExecutableFile on Windows
target.body:
## Summary

- `isExecutableFile` hardcoded `undefined` when calling `resolveWindowsExecutableExtSet`, ignoring any caller-provided custom `env.PATHEXT`
- `resolveExecutablePath` and `resolveExecutableFromPathEnv` would fall back to `process.env.PATHEXT` even when the caller supplied a different `env` with an extended `PATHEXT` (e.g. `.PS1`)
- This affects Windows deployments using sandbox/container environments where `PATHEXT` differs from `process.env` (Docker Windows containers, CI runners, tests)

## Fix

- Added optional `options.env` parameter to `isExecutableFile`
- `resolveWindowsExecutableExtSet` now reads from `options?.env` instead of `undefined`
- All 3 callers pass their available env through
- Fully backward compatible: undefined env falls back to `process.env.PATHEXT`, preserving existing behavior

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `isExecutableFile` ignored caller env.PATHEXT, causing false negatives when custom env has extended PATHEXT (e.g. .PS1 in containers).

**Real environment tested:** Linux (tests pass on CI-equivalent Node 22). Windows path verified via mock and static analysis — the fix follows the same `env?.PATHEXT ?? process.env.PATHEXT` pattern already used by `resolveWindowsExecutableExtensions` and `resolveWindowsExecutableExtSet` itself.

**Tests and validation:** `pnpm test src/infra/executable-path.test.ts` — 11 passed, 1 skipped (Windows-only test on Linux). The 3 unrelated `errors.test.ts` failures are pre-existing on main.

**Risk checklist:**
- User-facing behavior change: No (backward compatible, undefined env = same behavior)
- Config/API surface change: Yes — `isExecutableFile` signature changed (added optional param)
- Security impact: No
- Breaking for plugins: No (existing callers with single argument still work)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
target.author: wendy-chsy
